_comment: HIMMEL-1040 named plugin-profile registry. Resolved by plugin-profiles.mjs into an enabledPlugins map, injected per-dispatch via `claude --settings` (lever-b, highest non-managed precedence) so lane workers run lean while the operator's own ~/.claude stays full. Design: handovers/yotamleo/himmel/specs/design/2026-07-16-himmel-1040-agent-provider-plugin-profiles-design.md. FLOOR is inviolable (harness-operational: dispatch/search/handover/codex). CATALOG is the known plugin universe the resolver sets false for anything a profile does not enable — a COMPLETE map is correct whether Claude Code merges or replaces enabledPlugins. DENY-BY-DEFAULT BEYOND THE CATALOG: the resolver also disables every id passed via opts.installed (the caller's LIVE plugin universe — the spawn scripts pass readEnabledPluginIds over the child's effective config dir + every ancestor settings layer), so a plugin installed/enabled on the machine but absent from CATALOG is still turned off for a lane. Only a CATALOG-ONLY caller (one that passes no opts.installed) retains the staleness gap — such an id stays unmentioned and inherits its enabled state until added to CATALOG (the HIMMEL-819 class; a pre-commit floor/catalog check is the tracked follow-up).
